commands.review_summary:
The PR changes diagnostic session attention so warning/event backoff can stay throttled while recovery-eligible stuck sessions still return a classification, and updates diagnostic tests for throttled recovery ticks.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +46. Total +63 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can return `undefined` from `logSessionAttention` during warning backoff before the heartbeat reaches `requestStuckSessionRecovery`; I did not run a live QQ gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer proof review; automation cannot supply the external contributor's real-environment proof for this PR.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): keep recovery scheduling out of the stuck-session warning backoff
target.body:
## What

Diagnostic stuck-session recovery is no longer gated by the warning-backoff
throttle. `logSessionAttention` previously returned `undefined` whenever a
`session.stuck` or `session.long_running` classification fell inside its
exponential warning-backoff window. The heartbeat only schedules recovery from
a returned classification, so throttling the *warning* also throttled
*recovery*: a recovery-eligible lane could sit unrecovered until the next warn
interval (which doubles each time).

This change separates the two concerns. The warning backoff still suppresses
the repeated log line and diagnostic event, but a recovery-eligible
classification is now always returned so the heartbeat can call
`requestStuckSessionRecovery` on every tick. In-flight recovery requests remain
de-duplicated by the recovery coordinator, so this does not add redundant abort
work â€” it only removes the delay before the first (and each subsequent) recovery
attempt.

## Why

Reported in #92742: a stale active run blocked the `agent:main:direct` lane for
~17 minutes. Diagnostics detected the stuck session, but the same warning
backoff path suppressed the follow-up recovery checks, so the lane was not
released promptly even though the stale work was recoverable.

Root cause (`src/logging/diagnostic.ts`):

- `logSessionAttention` computes `recoveryEligible`, then for `session.stuck`
  and `session.long_running` does `if (params.ageMs < nextWarnAgeMs) return undefined;`
  *before* returning the classification.
- The heartbeat schedules recovery only when `logSessionAttention` returns a
  classification (`classification?.recoveryEligible` / `isActiveAbortRecoveryEligible`),
  so a throttled warning silently throttles recovery too.

## Change

- `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`: within the warning-backoff window, return early
  only when the session is **not** recovery-eligible. When it is recovery-eligible,
  suppress just the warning log + event and still return the classification.
  The `lastStuckWarnAgeMs` / `lastLongRunningWarnAgeMs` markers now advance only
  when a warning is actually emitted, so the backoff cadence is preserved.
- `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`: add a regression test proving recovery is
  still scheduled on a throttled tick, and update the existing backoff test
  (which had encoded the old coupling) to assert recovery now runs each tick
  while warnings stay throttled.

Scope is intentionally narrow. The QQ fast-ack / quick-reply path and the
hook-process watchdog mentioned in the issue are separate follow-ups, as the
triage review also noted.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Recovery-eligible stuck / long-running sessions
were not being recovered while the diagnostic warning backoff was active, so a
blocked lane stayed wedged until the next (exponentially growing) warn interval â€”
the ~17 minute direct-lane stall reported in #92742.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at current `main`
(`45e36a24`), Node v24.14.0, pnpm 11.2.2, macOS arm64. The defect is in the
diagnostic heartbeat control flow, so I exercised the real runtime path â€”
`startDiagnosticHeartbeat` driving `logSessionAttention` â†’ `requestStuckSessionRecovery`
on the 30s heartbeat â€” rather than a mocked stand-in. Only the terminal
`recoverStuckSession` side effect is stubbed so the lane-release request can be
observed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran the real heartbeat over a
recovery-eligible stuck session past the first warn (60s) and into the backoff
tick (90s) with:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix:** copied live terminal output from the `node` command above:

```
 RUN  v4.1.7  /Users/.../work/openclaw
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  91 passed (91)
```

For contrast, on unmodified `main` the same heartbeat path leaves the lane
unrecovered on the throttled tick â€” copied live output of the new regression
test before the patch:

```
 Ă— keeps scheduling recovery for a recovery-eligible stuck session while warnings are throttled
   AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" to be called 2 times, but got 1 times
   âťŻ src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts:524
```

**Observed result after fix:** on the throttled 90s heartbeat tick the warning
log/event stays suppressed (still one `session.stuck` warning), but
`requestStuckSessionRecovery` now fires â€” the observed recovery-request count
goes from 1 to 2 across that tick. In production this means the wedged lane gets
a recovery attempt on the very next ~30s heartbeat instead of waiting out the
doubled warn interval.

**What was not tested:** I did not stand up a live QQ gateway to reproduce the
original end-to-end 17-minute stall (no QQ environment available). The defect and
fix are reproduced deterministically at the diagnostic heartbeat level by the
command above, which drives the exact `logSessionAttention` â†’ recovery scheduling
path that failed.

Fixes #92742
target.author: gnanam1990
